---
title: "S0 Unit: Two-State Opposition"
---

S0 is the smallest finite layer in the book: two states and a flip between them.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Unit Goal

Learn the idea of opposition before learning full circular motion. S0 prepares the vocabulary for signs, paired states, poles, antipodes, and later sphere scaffolds.

:::

## The Idea

With only two points, there is not much room for motion. But there is one important action: switch to the opposite point.

::: {.definition-box}

## Definition In Words

An S0 opposition system has two states. Applying the flip once moves to the other state; applying it twice returns to the starting state.

<p class="formula-line">flip(flip(x)) = x</p>

:::

This is useful because many later ideas have a paired form: positive/negative, north/south, a point and its antipode, or a sign ambiguity in a rotation representation.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why is S0 useful even though it has only two points?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="source-trail">

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S0-T0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="S0-0001"></div>

</div>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="../../reader_path.qmd">Previous: Guided Learning Path</a>
<a href="opposition.qmd">Next: Two-Point Flip</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
